commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: The word This is full of declaration of virtue and praise of person. For he that was sent, he says, from God, he that with reason struck with astonishment the whole of Judaea, by the gravity of his life and its marvellous exercise in virtue, he that is fore-announced by the voice of the holy Prophets: called by Isaiah, The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, and by the blessed David, a lamp fore-ordained for Christ; This man came for a witness to hear witness of the Light. He here calls God the Word Light, and shews that He is One and strictly the very actual Light, with Whom there is by nature nought else that has the property of illumining, and that is not lacking light. Therefore foreign and, so to say, of other nature than the creature is the Word of God, since verily and truly is He strictly Light, the creature participate of light. He then that is unclassed with things made, and conceived of therefore as being of other nature than they, how will He be originate, rather how will He not be within the limits of Deity and replete with the Good Nature of Him who begat Him.
commentaries.content_en: The word This is full of declaration of virtue and praise of person. For he that was sent, he says, from God, he that with reason struck with astonishment the whole of Judaea, by the gravity of his life and its marvellous exercise in virtue, he that is fore-announced by the voice of the holy Prophets: called by Isaiah, The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, and by the blessed David, a lamp fore-ordained for Christ; This man came for a witness to hear witness of the Light. He here calls God the Word Light, and shews that He is One and strictly the very actual Light, with Whom there is by nature nought else that has the property of illumining, and that is not lacking light. Therefore foreign and, so to say, of other nature than the creature is the Word of God, since verily and truly is He strictly Light, the creature participate of light. He then that is unclassed with things made, and conceived of therefore as being of other nature than they, how will He be originate, rather how will He not be within the limits of Deity and replete with the Good Nature of Him who begat Him.
commentaries.content_pt: A palavra "Este" está repleta de declaração de virtude e louvor à Pessoa. Pois aquele que foi enviado, diz ele, de Deus, aquele que com razão impressionou toda a Judeia, pela gravidade de sua vida e seu maravilhoso exercício em virtude, aquele que é preanunciado pela voz dos santos Profetas: chamado por Isaías, A voz do que clama no deserto, e pelo abençoado Davi, uma lâmpada preordenada para Cristo; Este homem veio para dar testemunho, para que pudesse dar testemunho da Luz. Aqui, Ele chama Deus de Verbo Luz, e mostra que Ele é Um e estritamente a própria Luz atual, com a qual não há por natureza nada mais que tenha a propriedade de iluminar, e que não carece de luz. Portanto, é estrangeiro e, por assim dizer, de outra natureza do que a criatura, o Verbo de Deus, uma vez que verdadeiramente e realmente é Ele estritamente Luz, a criatura participa da luz. Portanto, aquele que não se classifica entre as coisas feitas, e é concebido, portanto, como sendo de outra natureza do que elas, como poderá Ele ser originado, antes, como não estará Ele dentro dos limites da Divindade e repleto da Boa Natureza daquele que O gerou.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: That all men might believe through him; i.e. by John the Baptist's preaching, who was God's instrument to induce them to believe in Jesus the Christ, or the Messias, their only Redeemer. (Witham)
commentaries.content_en: That all men might believe through him; i.e. by John the Baptist's preaching, who was God's instrument to induce them to believe in Jesus the Christ, or the Messias, their only Redeemer. (Witham)
commentaries.content_pt: Para que todos os homens pudessem crer por meio dele; isto é, pela pregação de João Batista, que foi o instrumento de Deus para induzi-los a crer em Jesus, o Cristo, ou o Messias, seu único Redentor. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: What is this, perhaps one may say, the servant bear witness to his Master. When then you see Him not only witnessed to by His servant, but even coming to him, and with Jews baptized by him, will you not be still more astonished and perplexed. Yet you ought not to be troubled nor confused, but amazed at such unspeakable goodness. Though if any still continue bewildered and confused, He will say to such an one what He said to John, Suffer it to be so now, for thus it becomes us to fulfill all righteousness Matthew 3:15; and, if any be still further troubled, again He will say to him too what he said to the Jews, But I receive not testimony from man. John 5:34 If now he needs not this witness, why was John sent from God. Not as though He required his testimony— this were extremest blasphemy. John himself informs us, when he says, That all men through him might believe. And Christ also, after having said that I receive not testimony from man John 5:34, in order that He may not seem to the foolish to clash with Himself, by declaring at one time There is another that bears witness of Me and I know that his witness is true John 5:32 (for He pointed to John;) and at another, I receive not testimony from man John 5:34; He immediately adds the solution of the doubt, But these things I say for your own sake, that you might be saved. As though He had said, that I am God, and the really-Begotten Son of God, and am of that Simple and Blessed Essence, I need none to witness to Me; and even though none would do so, yet am not I by this anything diminished in My Essence; but because I care for the salvation of the many, I have descended to such humility as to commit the witness of Me to a man. For by reason of the groveling nature and infirmity of the Jews, the faith in Him would in this way be more easily received, and more palatable. As then He clothed Himself with flesh, that he might not, by encountering men with the unveiled Godhead, destroy them all; so He sent forth a man for His herald, that those who heard might at the hearing of a kindred voice approach more readily. For (to prove) that He had no need of that (herald's) testimony, it would have sufficed that He should only have shown Himself who He was in His unveiled Essence, and have confounded them all. But this He did not for the reason I have before mentioned. He would have annihilated all, since none could have endured the encounter of that unapproachable light. Wherefore, as I said, He put on flesh, and entrusted the witness (of Himself) to one of our fellow-servants, since He arranged all for the salvation of men, looking not only to His own honor, but also to what might be readily received by, and be profitable to, His hearers. Which He glanced at when He said, These things I say for your sake, that you might be saved. John 5:34 And the Evangelist using the same language as his Master, after saying, to bear witness of that Light, adds, That all men through Him might believe. All but saying, Think not that the reason why John the Baptist came to bear witness, was that he might add anything to the trustworthiness of his Master. No; (He came,) that by his means beings of his own class might believe. For it is clear from what follows, that he used this expression in his anxiety to remove this suspicion beforehand, since he adds,.
commentaries.content_en: What is this, perhaps one may say, the servant bear witness to his Master. When then you see Him not only witnessed to by His servant, but even coming to him, and with Jews baptized by him, will you not be still more astonished and perplexed. No; (He came,) that by his means beings of his own class might believe. For it is clear from what follows, that he used this expression in his anxiety to remove this suspicion beforehand, since he adds,.
commentaries.content_pt: O que é isso, talvez alguém possa dizer, o servo dá testemunho ao seu Senhor. Quando, então, você vê que Ele não apenas é testemunhado por Seu servo, mas até mesmo vem até ele, e com os judeus batizados por ele, você não ficará ainda mais surpreso e perplexo? No entanto, você não deve estar angustiado nem confuso, mas maravilhado com tamanha bondade indescritível. Embora, se alguém ainda continuar perplexo e confuso, Ele dirá a tal pessoa o que disse a João: Deixe ser assim agora, pois assim nos convém cumprir toda a justiça. Mateus 3:15; e, se alguém estiver ainda mais angustiado, novamente Ele dirá a ele também o que disse aos judeus: Mas eu não recebo testemunho de homem. João 5:34 Se agora Ele não precisa desse testemunho, por que João foi enviado de Deus? Não como se Ele precisasse de seu testemunho — isso seria a mais extrema blasfêmia. O próprio João nos informa, quando diz: Para que todos os homens através dele pudessem crer. E Cristo também, depois de ter dito que eu não recebo testemunho de homem João 5:34, para que não pareça aos tolos que Ele está em contradição consigo mesmo, ao declarar em um momento: Há outro que dá testemunho de Mim e eu sei que seu testemunho é verdadeiro João 5:32 (pois Ele apontou para João); e em outro momento: Eu não recebo testemunho de homem João 5:34; Ele imediatamente acrescenta a solução da dúvida: Mas estas coisas eu digo por causa de vocês, para que possam ser salvos. Como se Ele tivesse dito, que eu sou Deus, e o realmente Gerado Filho de Deus, e sou daquela Essência Simples e Abençoada, não preciso de ninguém para testemunhar de Mim; e mesmo que ninguém o fizesse, não sou por isso diminuído em Minha Essência; mas porque me importo com a salvação dos muitos, desci a tal humildade a ponto de confiar o testemunho de Mim a um homem. Pois, devido à natureza rastejante e à fraqueza dos judeus, a fé Nele seria assim mais facilmente recebida e mais palatável. Assim como Ele se revestiu de carne, para que não, ao encontrar os homens com a Divindade descoberta, os destruísse a todos; assim Ele enviou um homem como Seu arauto, para que aqueles que ouvissem pudessem, ao ouvir uma voz semelhante, se aproximar mais prontamente. Pois (para provar) que Ele não precisava daquele testemunho (do arauto), teria sido suficiente que Ele apenas tivesse mostrado quem Ele era em Sua Essência descoberta, e os teria confundido a todos. Mas isso Ele não fez pela razão que mencionei anteriormente. Ele teria aniquilado a todos, uma vez que ninguém poderia ter suportado o encontro daquela luz inacessível. Portanto, como eu disse, Ele vestiu-se de carne, e confiou o testemunho (de Si mesmo) a um de nossos companheiros de serviço, uma vez que Ele organizou tudo para a salvação dos homens, não apenas olhando para Sua própria honra, mas também para o que poderia ser prontamente recebido e ser proveitoso para Seus ouvintes. O que Ele aludiu quando disse: Estas coisas eu digo por causa de vocês, para que possam ser salvos. João 5:34 E o Evangelista, usando a mesma linguagem que seu Senhor, após dizer, para dar testemunho daquela Luz, acrescenta: Para que todos os homens através dele pudessem crer. Quase dizendo: Não pense que a razão pela qual João Batista veio dar testemunho foi para que ele pudesse acrescentar algo à confiabilidade de seu Senhor. Não; (Ele veio,) para que por meio dele seres de sua própria classe pudessem crer. Pois é claro a partir do que se segue, que ele usou essa expressão em sua ansiedade para remover essa suspeita antecipadamente, uma vez que acrescenta.

commentaries.author: Theophylact of Ochrid
commentaries.content: This man was sent by God in order to bear witness of the Light. Lest anyone imagine that the Only-begotten was in need of this witness because He Himself lacked in something, the Evangelist immediately follows his statement, that John came to bear witness to the Son of God, with the reason, namely, that all men through him might believe. Did all men in fact believe through him? No. In what sense, then, did the Evangelist say, that all men ...might believe? As much as it was his part to do, John gave witness in order to draw all men to the Light; if some did not believe, he is not to blame. The sun rises each day for the purpose of giving light to all. If a man shuts himself up in a gloomy dwelling and does not enjoy the sunâ€™s rays, is the sun at fault? So it is here. John was sent so that all might believe. It is not his fault that it turned out otherwise.
commentaries.content_en: This man was sent by God in order to bear witness of the Light. Lest anyone imagine that the Only-begotten was in need of this witness because He Himself lacked in something, the Evangelist immediately follows his statement, that John came to bear witness to the Son of God, with the reason, namely, that all men through him might believe. Did all men in fact believe through him? No. In what sense, then, did the Evangelist say, that all men ...might believe? As much as it was his part to do, John gave witness in order to draw all men to the Light; if some did not believe, he is not to blame. The sun rises each day for the purpose of giving light to all. If a man shuts himself up in a gloomy dwelling and does not enjoy the sunâ€™s rays, is the sun at fault? So it is here. John was sent so that all might believe. It is not his fault that it turned out otherwise.
commentaries.content_pt: Este homem foi enviado por Deus para dar testemunho da Luz. Para que ninguém imagine que o Unigênito estava necessitado desse testemunho porque Ele mesmo carecia de algo, o Evangelista imediatamente segue sua afirmação, que João veio dar testemunho ao Filho de Deus, com a razão, a saber, que todos os homens através dele pudessem crer. Todos os homens de fato creram através dele? Não. Em que sentido, então, o Evangelista disse que todos os homens ...poderiam crer? Na medida em que era seu dever, João deu testemunho para atrair todos os homens à Luz; se alguns não creram, ele não é culpado. O sol nasce a cada dia com o propósito de dar luz a todos. Se um homem se tranca em uma moradia sombria e não desfruta dos raios do sol, é o sol que está em falta? Assim é aqui. João foi enviado para que todos pudessem crer. Não é culpa dele que tenha resultado de outra forma.
